content.full_text:
March 20, 1992

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Request for Opinion
VIA FAX# 916 327-2026

Dear Ms. Breeze,

As we discussed on the telephone Tuesday, I am a Member of the City Council in Coronado. I own a rental property in Coronado which is zoned Central Commercial and which will be affected by decisions that will be coming before the City Council in the next few months. (I also own a five-unit residential rental property in the R-3 multi-family zone and my own residence in the R-1A single-family zone in Coronado.)

Coronado is a small city across the bay from San Diego. The 1990 census reported that Coronado's population is about 26,500 people which includes approximately 9,800 people residing in military group quarters. The Central Commercial (CC) zone includes 358 parcels totalling 14.93 acres. There is also a Limited Commercial (LC) zone which includes 40 parcels totalling 8.03 acres. Both zones permit general commercial uses typical of central business district areas. Individual parcels vary in size and shape. (This information was provided to me by Mr. Scott Huth of the city's Community Development Department.)

The property I own in the Central Commercial zone is a single lot of approximately 3,920 square feet in size (less than one-tenth of one acre) and is improved with two older one-story buildings of approximately 750 and 1,880 square feet each. The building addresses are 1012 and 1016 Isabella Avenue. The smaller building (1012 Isabella) is leased to a dentist and the other building is leased to a beauty salon operator.

In the near future, the city will be considering two items which will affect my property. The first item affecting my property is the imposition of a trash collection fee. A city-wide fee is being considered and one proposal would assess commercial properties at a different rate than residential properties. If all commercial properties are affected similarly, may I participate?

The second item affecting my property is consideration of changes in the permitted uses within the Central Commercial zone (ie. permitting "mixed use" of both residential and commercial uses on the same property, possible changes to parking standards, possible changes to building design standards, etc.). If all properties in the Central Commercial zone

March 20, 1992

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission

are affected similarly, may I participate? If all properties in both the Central Commercial and Limited Commercial zones are affected similarly, may I participate?

I look forward to receiving your opinion on these issues. If you require additional information or have any questions, please do not hesitate to call me. Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Robert W. Chamberlain
Councilmember

cc: City attorney

This is a map of the City of Coronado, Area 01, showing a detailed street layout with labeled streets and landmarks such as the Golf Course, Gloriett, Beach, and various neighborhoods. There is a marked area labeled "Subject" with an arrow pointing to it. The map is dated MAR-23-1992 at 13:22 and is from the City of Coronado.
